entities.name: SORGHUM BICOLOR
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor, the crop species studied for pollen-mediated gene flow.

entities.name: POLLEN MEDIATED GENE FLOW
entities.description: The movement of genes through pollen, studied in the context of sorghum varietal purity and transgenic risk mitigation.

entities.name: VARIETAL PURITY
entities.description: Maintaining the genetic consistency of a crop variety.

entities.name: TRANSGENIC GENE FLOW
entities.description: The transfer of genes from genetically modified organisms to other plants.

entities.name: KENYA
entities.description: The country where the replicated trials were conducted.

entities.name: OCHUTI
entities.description: A local sorghum landrace used as the pollen donor.

entities.name: ANTHER DEHISCENCE
entities.description: The release of pollen from the anther, correlated with vapour pressure deficit.

entities.name: VAPOUR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: A measure of atmospheric dryness, correlated with anther dehiscence.

entities.name: DISTANCE
entities.description: Spatial separation between pollen source and receiver plants, impacting PMGF frequency.

entities.name: FEMALE FLORETS
entities.description: The receptive parts of the sorghum plant that receive pollen.

entities.name: MALE STERILE PLANTS
entities.description: Plants incapable of producing pollen, used as pollen baits.

entities.name: POLLEN
entities.description: The carrier of genetic material for plant reproduction.

entities.name: SELF-PRODUCED POLLEN
entities.description: Pollen originating from the same plant, reducing the likelihood of cross-pollination.
